title: Theoretical Landscape Survey: Dark Matter Models vs Observed m=2

models.name: Cold Dark Matter (CDM)
models.consistency: CONSISTENT — CDM naturally produces triaxial halos with m=2 distortions
models.testable_via: Predicts m=2 power correlated with merger history/environment

models.name: Self-Interacting Dark Matter (SIDM, σ/m ~ 1 cm²/g)
models.consistency: TESTABLE — predicts specific radial m=2 gradient different from CDM
models.testable_via: RADIAL GRADIENT: inner m=2 / outer m=2 ratio should be LOW

models.name: Fuzzy Dark Matter (ψDM, m_a ~ 10⁻²² eV)
models.consistency: DISTINGUISHABLE — different m-spectrum from CDM triaxiality
models.testable_via: M-SPECTRUM: expect comparable power at m=3,4 relative to m=2

models.name: Warm Dark Matter (WDM, m ~ few keV)
models.consistency: CONSISTENT but not distinguishable from CDM
models.testable_via: Hard to distinguish from CDM with m=2 alone at these masses

models.name: MOND + External Field Effect (EFE)
models.testable_via: ALIGNMENT TEST: m=2 PA should correlate with nearest neighbor direction

discriminating_tests.name: Test A: Radial m=2 gradient (inner vs outer)
discriminating_tests.feasibility: HIGH — can compute from existing data

discriminating_tests.name: Test B: m-spectrum shape (m=1,2,3,4 relative power)
discriminating_tests.feasibility: HIGH — can compute from existing data

discriminating_tests.name: Test C: m=2 PA alignment with environment
Test D: m=2 vs stellar mass/concentration
discriminating_tests.feasibility: MEDIUM — need cross-match with morphological catalogs

verdict: CDM consistent; SIDM, FDM, MOND testable with existing data
immediate_next: Phase 11.2: Radial m=2 gradient (CDM vs SIDM)
Phase 11.4: PA alignment with environment (CDM vs MOND)
